subject: Mathematics
title: Quantum Threats to Cryptography
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Quantum Threats to Cryptography.

hook.text: Quantum computers could one day factor huge numbers in hours, cracking RSA wide open.

buildup.text: Shor's algorithm, designed for quantum machines, factors integers exponentially faster.

discovery.text: Current quantum computers are too small, but they're growing rapidly each year.

twist.text: Cryptographers aren't waiting. Lattice-based algorithms are already being standardized.

climax.text: The race between code makers and code breakers has entered the quantum era.

punchline.text: Tomorrow's computers may crack today's secrets.

quiz.question: Why might quantum computers threaten current encryption?
options: They could factor large numbers quickly
